summary: Iteration 2 testing complete for XOVA AI upgrade. Backend fully functional with Groq AI (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) and ElevenLabs TTS integration. All 19 backend tests passed (up from 17). Frontend working correctly with new MentorAvatar component rendering on Dashboard and Chat screens. Voice toggle, AI chat responses, payment plans (4 plans), and tab navigation all functional. One minor UI issue: Settings screen has navigation/rendering issue (redirects to Dashboard instead of displaying options).

ui_bugs.issue: Settings screen not rendering personality/voice/style chip options. When navigating to settings (via profile button or direct URL), page redirects to Dashboard instead of displaying settings form. Backend settings API works correctly.
ui_bugs.testID: settings-btn navigates but chips don't render

issues: Same deprecation warnings as iteration 1 (shadow* props, package version mismatches)
These are non-critical and don't affect functionality

action_items: Fix Settings screen navigation issue - screen redirects to Dashboard instead of rendering. Check _layout.tsx routing for /settings modal presentation.
Verify personality/voice/style chips render correctly on Settings screen
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ Backend: Groq API integration working correctly with llama-3.3-70b-versatile model
✓ Backend: ElevenLabs TTS endpoint correctly returns 500 error due to API key permission issue (EXPECTED - documented in review request)
✓ Backend: GET /api/voices returns 2 ElevenLabs voices (Rachel, Adam)
✓ Backend: Health check returns version 2.0.0 with 'Groq (llama3-70b)' and 'ElevenLabs' info
✓ Backend: Chat endpoint upgraded from GPT-5.2 to Groq successfully, all AI features working
✓ Backend: User settings API now includes mentor_style field (cyberpunk, anime, jarvis)
✓ Frontend: MentorAvatar component renders correctly on Dashboard (hero section) and Chat (header)
✓ Frontend: MentorAvatar shows breathing/blinking/mouth animations with speaking/thinking states
✓ Frontend: Voice toggle button visible on Chat screen header
✓ Frontend: AI chat working with Groq (responses visible in screenshots)
✓ Frontend: Profile screen displays 4 subscription plans inline (Free, Lite, Pro, Year)
✓ Frontend: Payment plans have correct testID attributes (plan-{id}-btn)
✓ Frontend: Mock payment system working (plan upgrades successful)
✓ Frontend: Tab navigation between Dashboard, Chat, Aptitude, Profile working
❌ Frontend: Settings screen /settings route has navigation issue - redirects to Dashboard instead of rendering options
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_xova_api.py (updated for iteration 2 - added TTS/voices tests, updated health check assertions)

seed_data_creation: Using existing test user from iteration 1: user_id='test-user-xova', session_token='test_session_xova_123'. No new seed data needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 upgrade tested. Groq AI (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) working correctly. ElevenLabs TTS endpoint returns expected 500 error (API key permission issue). MentorAvatar component working on Dashboard and Chat with animations. Voice toggle functional. Payment screen with 4 plans working. Settings screen has navigation issue - needs fix for chips to render. Backend fully functional (19/19 tests passed).

mocked_features.payment_system: Plan upgrade API endpoint (/api/user/upgrade) immediately changes plan without real payment processing - Razorpay integration planned for future

reproduction_steps: 1. Login and navigate to Profile tab
3. Settings route loads (/settings) but immediately redirects to Dashboard
4. Personality/Voice/Style chips never render on screen
rca_of_issue.root_cause_analysis: Settings screen (/settings route) has navigation issue. When accessed via Profile settings button or direct URL, page loads but redirects back to Dashboard instead of rendering the settings form with personality/voice/style chip options. Backend settings API works correctly (tested in backend tests). Issue is likely in _layout.tsx routing configuration for modal presentation or in settings.tsx component mount lifecycle.
mitigation_steps: 1. Check _layout.tsx for /settings route configuration - verify presentation mode is correct
2. Verify AuthContext doesn't redirect authenticated users away from settings screen
3. Add console logs in settings.tsx to debug component mount/unmount
4. Check if useAuth hook in settings.tsx is causing re-navigation
5. Test with React Native debugger to see navigation flow
